title: Low-level environmental lead exposure in childhood and adult intellectual function: a follow-up study.
ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective cohort study, not based on electronic health records.
summary: A follow-up study assessing the relationship between early life environmental lead exposure and intellectual function (IQ) in adulthood.
key_findings: Childhood blood-lead concentration (mean of ages 4 and 10 years) had the strongest relationship with Full-Scale IQ.
Significant associations found for blood-lead concentrations at 6 months, 4 years, and 10 years.
population: Adults in the Boston area who were part of a prospective cohort as newborns and children
limitations: Potential confounding effects of maternal IQ
